title_en: Is Aung San Suu Kyi dead?
rubric_en: Myanmar’s jailed leader has not been seen since 2022
rubric_zh: 这位被监禁的缅甸领导人自2022年以来再未公开露面

paragraphs.en: TAICHITO HAD followed Aung San Suu Kyi everywhere. But the dog was not allowed to accompany Myanmar’s leader into prison after a military junta overthrew her government five years ago. Last month, the floppy-eared mongrel died at her home in Yangon, the former capital, aged 15, still awaiting her return. He had been a gift from Kim Aris, her son, on her release from an earlier spell in prison in 2010. “I think he was the best thing I ever gave her. He was very faithful to her,” says Mr Aris in an interview in London.
paragraphs.zh: 泰奇托曾经寸步不离地跟着昂山素季。但五年前，一个军政府推翻她的政府后，这只狗未被允许陪同缅甸领导人入狱。上个月，这只耷拉着耳朵的混种犬在前首都仰光她的家中去世，享年15岁，至死仍在等待她归来。它是她的儿子金·阿里斯在她2010年结束此前一段囚禁获释时送给她的礼物。“我觉得它是我送给她的最好的东西。它对她非常忠诚，”阿里斯在伦敦接受采访时说。

paragraphs.en: Mr Aris has a bigger worry, however: his aged mother has disappeared. He has been travelling the world in recent months, pushing presidents and ministers to demand that Myanmar’s military government provide “proof of life”. The last official appearance of Ms Suu Kyi, who would now be 81, came at the conclusion of her show trial at the end of 2022. Since then, the army has consistently refused her lawyers’ requests to see her. Reports of sightings in Myanmar’s prison system have trickled out of the war-torn country in recent years, but are impossible to verify.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，阿里斯有一个更大的担忧：他年迈的母亲失踪了。近几个月来，他一直在世界各地奔走，敦促各国总统和部长要求缅甸军政府提供“存活证明”。现年本应81岁的昂山素季最后一次官方露面，是在2022年底对她的作秀审判结束时。此后，军方一直拒绝她的律师提出的会见请求。近年来，关于她在缅甸监狱系统中被人看见的消息陆续从这个饱受战争蹂躏的国家传出，但无法核实。

paragraphs.en: Ms Suu Kyi ranks as one of the world’s most famous political prisoners. She spent many years under house arrest while leading peaceful democratic protests against Myanmar’s military regime during the 1980s to the 2010s, and won the Nobel peace prize in 1991. After her party finally came to power in 2015, however, her reputation was damaged by her defence of the armed forces’ treatment of the Rohingyas, a persecuted Muslim minority. In 2021 a military coup removed her from power, before sending her to prison.
paragraphs.zh: 昂山素季是世界上最著名的政治犯之一。20世纪80年代至2010年代，她领导反对缅甸军政府的和平民主抗议，期间多年遭软禁，并于1991年获得诺贝尔和平奖。然而，在她的政党终于于2015年上台后，她因维护武装部队对受迫害的穆斯林少数族群罗兴亚人的处置而名誉受损。2021年，一场军事政变将她赶下台，随后把她送进监狱。

paragraphs.en: The military government claimed in April that it had moved Ms Suu Kyi to house arrest, but has turned down repeated requests from diplomats who ask to visit. Pressed by diplomats about her condition, regime officials invariably reply that she is in good health, but say little else. A photo published at the time of her supposed move to house arrest showed her chatting with a policeman and an army officer inside an unidentifiable building. There is no indication that it is of recent vintage, however, and Mr Aris doubts its authenticity. He says that if she is indeed under house arrest, it is not at her home in Yangon and that her house in Naypyidaw, the new capital, has been torn down.
paragraphs.zh: 军政府4月声称已将昂山素季转为软禁，但多次拒绝外交官提出的探访请求。当外交官追问她的状况时，政权官员总是一成不变地回答说她身体健康，除此之外几乎不再透露。她据称转为软禁时公布的一张照片显示，她在一栋无法辨认的建筑内与一名警察和一名军官交谈。然而，没有迹象表明这张照片是近期拍摄的，阿里斯也怀疑其真实性。他说，如果她确实遭到软禁，也不是在她位于仰光的家中；而她在新首都内比都的住所已经被拆除。

paragraphs.en: Senior General Min Aung Hlaing, the man who led the coup against her, had himself declared president in March. He has been asked about Ms Suu Kyi at least twice recently. Narendra Modi, India’s prime minister, brought her up in talks with him in Delhi last month. And in May, Julie Bishop, the UN special envoy on Myanmar, asked to see her when she met the general. Diplomats privy to those conversations say that he responded with anger at the mention of her name.
paragraphs.zh: 敏昂莱大将，也就是领导针对她发动政变的人，已于3月宣布自己出任总统。最近至少有两次，有人向他问起昂山素季。印度总理纳伦德拉·莫迪上个月在德里与他会谈时提到了她。5月，联合国缅甸问题特使朱莉·毕晓普会见这名将军时，也要求见她。知情外交官说，他一听到她的名字就愤怒回应。

paragraphs.en: Some of those briefed on these conversations worry that the coup-leader’s reaction could indicate that he is unable to produce the requested proof of life—because she is either dead or in bad shape. Others are sceptical. “To keep that under wraps would be impossible,” says Morgan Michaels, a Myanmar expert at the International Institute for Strategic Studies, a think-tank in London. The general’s profound dislike of his political rival, as one ambassador puts it, may be sufficient explanation for her isolation.
paragraphs.zh: 一些听取过这些谈话通报的人担心，政变领导人的反应可能表明他无法提供所要求的存活证明，因为她要么已经去世，要么状况很糟。也有人对此持怀疑态度。“要把这种事捂住是不可能的，”伦敦智库国际战略研究所的缅甸问题专家摩根·迈克尔斯说。正如一位大使所说，这名将军对其政治对手的深切厌恶，或许已足以解释她为何被隔绝。

paragraphs.en: Foreign ministers from other members of the Association of South-East Asian Nations (ASEAN) have repeatedly raised Ms Suu Kyi’s welfare with their opposite number in the junta, including at a meeting in Bangkok on July 12th. The generals want to fully rejoin ASEAN, which suspended Myanmar from high-level meetings after the coup. They also want their seat at the UN. Myanmar has been represented there since the coup by an official of the ousted civilian government. Releasing Ms Suu Kyi, or just giving foreign diplomats access to her, might melt opposition in both institutions to normalisation.
paragraphs.zh: 东南亚国家联盟（东盟）其他成员国的外长曾多次向军政府中与他们对口的官员提及昂山素季的安危，包括在7月12日曼谷的一次会议上。将军们希望全面重返东盟；政变后，东盟暂停缅甸参加高级别会议。他们还想要缅甸在联合国的席位。政变以来，缅甸在联合国一直由被推翻的文官政府的一名官员代表。释放昂山素季，或者仅仅允许外国外交官接触她，都可能软化这两个机构中反对关系正常化的立场。

paragraphs.en: Doing either would have less predictable consequences inside Myanmar. Ms Suu Kyi has consistently advocated non-violent resistance. Since the coup, a nationwide campaign of armed groups representing many of Myanmar’s ethnic minorities has emerged to challenge military rule; for the first time they have joined forces with revolutionaries from the Burman ethnic majority. But Ms Suu Kyi, a Burman nationalist who in office gave minorities short shrift, lacks support from many armed groups. Freeing her would be “the easiest way to break up” unity among them, says a foreign diplomat who has been working to stitch them together.
paragraphs.zh: 这两种做法中的任何一种，在缅甸国内造成的后果都不那么可预测。昂山素季一直主张非暴力抵抗。政变以来，一场由代表缅甸许多少数民族的武装组织组成的全国性运动兴起，挑战军方统治；他们首次与来自缅族多数族群的革命者联手。但昂山素季是一名缅族民族主义者，执政时对少数族群重视不足，因此缺乏许多武装组织的支持。一名一直在努力把这些组织联合起来的外国外交官说，释放她将是“拆散”它们团结的“最容易办法”。

paragraphs.en: Mr Michaels sees the armed forces as less worried about the armed uprising than they are about non-violent movements of the sort that Ms Suu Kyi has advocated since 1988. She retains an almost talismanic power over many members of the Burman majority, who continue to take personal risks to show their fealty.
paragraphs.zh: 迈克尔斯认为，相比武装起义，武装部队更担心昂山素季自1988年以来一直倡导的那类非暴力运动。她对许多缅族多数族群成员仍保有近乎护身符般的影响力，这些人继续冒着个人风险来表示对她的忠诚。

paragraphs.en: The diplomatic focus on Ms Suu Kyi’s plight risks eclipsing that of Myanmar’s 55m people, who continue to suffer under the junta. She is but one of 14,517 political prisoners confirmed to be held by the government, says the Assistance Association for Political Prisoners (AAPP), an advocacy group. Medical care in prison is poor, air conditioning in the sweltering hot season unavailable. (Ms Suu Kyi is reported to have refused an air-conditioned cell because others do not have one.) This year alone, the AAPP confirmed the deaths of over 60 political prisoners in custody. Even as Mr Aris seeks news of his own mother, he says she would not want their plight to be forgotten. ■
paragraphs.zh: 外交界对昂山素季困境的关注，可能会让缅甸5500万人民的处境相形失色；他们仍在军政府统治下受苦。倡议组织政治犯援助协会（AAPP）表示，她只是已确认被政府关押的14,517名政治犯之一。监狱里的医疗条件很差，在酷热季节也没有空调。（据报道，昂山素季曾拒绝入住有空调的牢房，因为其他人没有。）仅今年一年，AAPP就确认有60多名政治犯死于羁押期间。即便阿里斯在寻找自己母亲的消息，他也说，她不会希望这些人的苦难被遗忘。■

paragraphs.en: For exclusive coverage of Asian politics, economics and security, sign up to Asia Bulletin, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如需获取关于亚洲政治、经济与安全的独家报道，请订阅《亚洲简报》，这是我们每周一期、仅面向订阅者的新闻简报。
